items.briefing:
A cyberattack on Mackay Sugar has raised concerns about vulnerabilities across Australia's food supply chain, highlighting that critical industries beyond finance and healthcare are at risk. Separately, new research warns that one in three IT assets in organisations lack basic security controls — meaning many devices and systems may be unprotected without staff realising it. Australians using illegal streaming services are also being warned they face increased exposure to cybercrime, including malware and financial fraud.

From 1 July, some text messages will look different due to new sender ID registration requirements — if you receive an unexpected SMS asking you to click a link or provide details, be especially cautious. **Tip:** If a text message looks unfamiliar or urgent, don't click any links — contact the sender directly using a number you already trust.

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Critical infrastructure attack on major Australian food exporter signals vulnerability of essential supply chains.
